definition: Agricultural supply shocks are primary inflation regime catalysts for EM economies (food CPI weights 30-50%, shock impact 1.5-3.0pp) but secondary for DM (food weights 10-15%, impact 0.3-0.5pp under anchored conditions), with export ban reflexivity providing non-linear amplification and biofuel mandates creating a post-2005 structural energy-food correlation of 0.4-0.6.
evidence: Food CPI weights of 30-50% in EM vs. 10-15% in DM create structural wedge
30% agricultural shock: 1.5-3.0pp in EM vs. 0.3-0.5pp in DM under anchored conditions
Biofuel mandates (40% of US corn, 30% of Brazilian sugarcane) create post-2005 energy-food correlation
Current stocks-to-use ratios below 10-year averages but above stress zones
Structural food price floor from elevated fertilizer costs (+40-60% vs. pre-2020)
open_questions: At what stocks-to-use threshold do agricultural conditions become regime-relevant for DM?
How much does the biofuel structural floor limit downside in agricultural commodity prices?
